comments.author: @LifeNest-3
comments.text: It's fascinating how the news only shows the Strait of Hormuz as a dangerous geopolitical choke point, but this video beautifully captures the quiet, human reality of the people who call it home. The contrast between the massive oil tankers and the traditional fishing boats is just incredible.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: I'm so glad the video was able to highlight that human element! It's exactly what I hoped to convey.

comments.author: @sepideh1111
comments.text:
I traveled with my family to southern Iran near the Persian Gulf as a child in spring. The nature there is remarkably unique. The Persian Gulf offers so much—many different types of fish and stunning bodies of water—yet since 1979, when the western world helped a terrorist group take over our country, we’ve lost nearly everything.

I can’t even believe that China has bought  Kish Island, and it makes me deeply worried about the environment. We won’t forgive and forget.

comments.author: @TopUnrealPlanet4K
comments.text: I’m honestly blown away by how immersive this video is. The pacing allows every moment to breathe, and that makes the entire experience feel so much more real. It doesn’t try to overwhelm, it simply draws you in. That quiet confidence is powerful. Watching it left me with a strong sense of curiosity, and we just uploaded a video that explores a similar atmosphere but reveals even deeper and more surprising details.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: I'm so glad you enjoyed the immersive experience and the pacing! That's exactly what I was aiming for with this video.
